mechanism_narrative: Rising real retail sales (RSAFS) over a 3-month window signals strengthening consumer demand and corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days through improved profit expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 357013M as of 2005-07-01 reflects robust consumer spending
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive RSAFS momentum fails to produce positive 20d returns in > 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 5.0% signals labor market tightening and wage pressure, which equity markets interpret as inflationary risk and reduced real returns, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances when UNRATE < 4.8%, or Rank IC > -0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) exceeds 3.5% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises month-over-month, the Fed is in restrictive mode. This joint tightening signal predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as equity valuations compress under higher discount rates and margin pressure.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 3.26% and CPIAUCSL at 194.1 as of 2005-07-01
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation acceleration
failure_condition: Interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over any 3-year window, or negative returns occur in < 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates above its 6-month moving average, signaling robust supply-side capacity utilization and productivity gains. This drives positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as markets reprrice earnings upward.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive INDPRO growth fails to produce positive 5d returns in > 52% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline by more than 10% month-over-month, signaling a contraction in residential investment demand. This forward-looking indicator predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as it foreshadows broader economic slowdown and reduced construction employment.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 55% of instances when HOUST declines > 10%, or Rank IC > -0.01 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates to below 100k new jobs per month while UNRATE remains stable, signaling labor market weakening. This divergence predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as markets anticipate earnings pressure from reduced consumer spending.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month growth < 100k AND UNRATE unchanged or rising
failure_condition: Interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression, or negative returns occur in < 48% of triggered instances over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerates above 3% annualized in the current quarter, signaling robust economic expansion. This broad-based demand strength produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through improved corporate earnings and reduced recession risk premium.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive GDP growth fails to produce positive 60d returns in > 58% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is above 3.0% and CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth exceeds 2.5%, real rates are restrictive. Combined with declining PAYEMS growth, this three-way signal indicates monetary policy is tightening into slowing labor demand, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 3.0% AND CPIAUCSL YoY > 2.5% AND PAYEMS growth < 150k
evidence_refs: fact:Restrictive monetary stance evident in 2005-07 snapshot
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with labor market softening
failure_condition: Multi-way interaction is not statistically significant (p > 0.15) in logistic regression, or negative returns occur in < 50% of triggered instances over any 3-year window.
